# 📦 Prisma Type Definitions & Generated Types Path

## 🎯 Quick Answer: Type Definition Locations

### **Primary Location (Auto-Generated):**
```
node_modules/.prisma/client/
├── index.d.ts                 ← Main type definitions (💾 ACID Types)
├── index.js                   ← JavaScript runtime
├── runtime/
│   ├── library.d.ts          ← Runtime type helpers
│   └── query.d.ts            ← Query builder types
└── prisma-client.d.ts        ← PrismaClient interface
```

### **Source Location (What you edit):**
```
backend/
├── prisma/
│   ├── schema.prisma          ← SOURCE (enums, models)
│   └── migrations/
│       └── [migration_name]/
│           └── migration.sql  ← Applied changes
└── src/
    └── prisma/
        └── prisma.service.ts  ← Our wrapper (uses types)
```

---

## 🔧 How Prisma Generates Types

### **Step 1: Define Models in schema.prisma**
```prisma
model IngredientBatch {
  id              String          @id @default(cuid())
  ingredientId    String
  storeId         String
  batchCode       String
  remainingQty    Float
  status          BatchStatus     @default(ACTIVE)
  createdAt       DateTime        @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime        @updatedAt
  
  // Relations
  ingredient      Ingredient      @relation(fields: [ingredientId], references: [id])
  store           Store           @relation(fields: [storeId], references: [id])
  adjustments     StockAdjustment[]
  
  @@unique([storeId, batchCode])
  @@index([storeId, status])
}

enum BatchStatus {
  ACTIVE
  SOFT_LOCKED
  DEPLETED
  EXPIRED
}
```

### **Step 2: Run Prisma Generate**
```bash
npm run prisma:generate
# or
npx prisma generate
```

### **Step 3: Prisma Creates index.d.ts**
TypeScript types are auto-generated in `node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts`

---

## 📄 Generated Type Definitions (From index.d.ts)

### **What Gets Generated:**

```typescript
// ============================================================
// FILE: node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
// AUTO-GENERATED BY: prisma generate
// ============================================================

// 1️⃣ MODEL TYPES
export interface IngredientBatch {
  id: string
  ingredientId: string
  storeId: string
  batchCode: string
  remainingQty: number
  status: BatchStatus
  createdAt: Date
  updatedAt: Date
}

// 2️⃣ ENUM TYPES
export enum BatchStatus {
  ACTIVE = "ACTIVE",
  SOFT_LOCKED = "SOFT_LOCKED",
  DEPLETED = "DEPLETED",
  EXPIRED = "EXPIRED",
}

// 3️⃣ INPUT TYPES (for create/update operations)
export type IngredientBatchCreateInput = {
  id?: string
  batchCode: string
  ingredientId: string
  storeId: string
  remainingQty: number
  status?: BatchStatus
  createdAt?: Date | string
  updatedAt?: Date | string
  ingredient: IngredientCreateNestedOneWithoutBatchesInput
  store: StoreCreateNestedOneWithoutBatchesInput
  adjustments?: StockAdjustmentCreateNestedManyWithoutBatchInput
}

// 4️⃣ UPDATE INPUT TYPES
export type IngredientBatchUpdateInput = {
  id?: StringFieldUpdateOperationsInput | string
  batchCode?: StringFieldUpdateOperationsInput | string
  remainingQty?: FloatFieldUpdateOperationsInput | number
  status?: EnumBatchStatusFieldUpdateOperationsInput | BatchStatus
  updatedAt?: DateTimeFieldUpdateOperationsInput | Date | string
  // ... more fields
}

// 5️⃣ WHERE CLAUSE TYPES (for filtering)
export type IngredientBatchWhereInput = {
  AND?: IngredientBatchWhereInput | IngredientBatchWhereInput[]
  OR?: IngredientBatchWhereInput[]
  NOT?: IngredientBatchWhereInput | IngredientBatchWhereInput[]
  id?: StringFilter | string
  storeId?: StringFilter | string
  batchCode?: StringFilter | string
  remainingQty?: FloatFilter | number
  status?: EnumBatchStatusFilter | BatchStatus
  createdAt?: DateTimeFilter | Date | string
  updatedAt?: DateTimeFilter | Date | string
  ingredient?: IngredientRelationFilter
  store?: StoreRelationFilter
  adjustments?: StockAdjustmentListRelationFilter
}

// 6️⃣ SELECT TYPES (for picking fields)
export type IngredientBatchSelect = {
  id?: boolean
  ingredientId?: boolean
  storeId?: boolean
  batchCode?: boolean
  remainingQty?: boolean
  status?: boolean
  ingredient?: boolean | IngredientArgs
  store?: boolean | StoreArgs
  adjustments?: boolean | StockAdjustmentFindManyArgs
  createdAt?: boolean
  updatedAt?: boolean
}

// 7️⃣ INCLUDE TYPES (for eager loading relations)
export type IngredientBatchInclude = {
  ingredient?: boolean | IngredientArgs
  store?: boolean | StoreArgs
  adjustments?: boolean | StockAdjustmentFindManyArgs
}

// 8️⃣ TRANSACTION CLIENT TYPE (FOR ACID!)
export interface Prisma$TransactionClient {
  ingredientBatch: PrismaPromise<BatchDelegate<...>>
  stockAdjustment: PrismaPromise<AdjustmentDelegate<...>>
  scanLog: PrismaPromise<ScanLogDelegate<...>>
  // ... all other models
}

// 9️⃣ TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVELS (FOR ACID!)
export enum TransactionIsolationLevel {
  ReadUncommitted = "ReadUncommitted",
  ReadCommitted = "ReadCommitted",
  RepeatableRead = "RepeatableRead",
  Serializable = "Serializable"
}

// 🔟 PRISMA CLIENT TRANSACTION METHOD
export interface PrismaClient {
  $transaction<T>(
    fn: (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => Promise<T>,
    options?: {
      isolationLevel?: Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel
      timeout?: number
      maxWait?: number
    }
  ): Promise<T>
}
```

---

## 🎯 How ACID Queries Use Generated Types

### **Example: Stock Adjustment with Type Safety**

```typescript
// File: backend/src/modules/stock-adjustments/stock-adjustments.service.ts

import { PrismaClient, Prisma, BatchStatus, StockAdjustmentType } from '@prisma/client';
// ↑ All types imported from node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts

async create(actorUserId: string, batchId: string, dto: CreateStockAdjustmentDto) {
  // Type 1: Direct model access (findUnique returns typed result)
  const batch: Prisma.IngredientBatch | null = await this.prisma.ingredientBatch.findUnique({
    where: { id: batchId },
    include: {
      ingredient: true,  // Type: IngredientBatchInclude
      store: true
    }
  });
  // ↑ TypeScript ensures 'batch' has all IngredientBatch properties

  // Type 2: Transaction parameter with Prisma.TransactionClient
  const result = await this.prisma.runInTransaction(async (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => {
    // Type 3: Within transaction, types are available
    const freshBatch = await tx.ingredientBatch.findUniqueOrThrow({
      where: { id: batchId }
    });
    // ↑ Type: Prisma.IngredientBatch (guaranteed to exist)

    // Type 4: Enum type checking
    if (
      dto.adjustmentType === StockAdjustmentType.DECREASE &&  // ← Enum type
      dto.quantity > freshBatch.remainingQty
    ) {
      throw appException(HttpStatus.CONFLICT, 'Exceeds remaining quantity');
    }

    // Type 5: Update with typed input
    const updatedBatch: Prisma.IngredientBatch = await tx.ingredientBatch.update({
      where: { id: batchId },
      data: {
        remainingQty: freshBatch.remainingQty - dto.quantity,
        status: BatchStatus.ACTIVE  // ← Type-checked enum
      }
    });

    // Type 6: Create with typed input
    const adjustment: Prisma.StockAdjustment = await tx.stockAdjustment.create({
      data: {
        storeId: freshBatch.storeId,
        batchId,
        adjustmentType: dto.adjustmentType,  // ← Enum type
        quantity: dto.quantity,
        reason: dto.reason,
        createdByUserId: actorUserId
      }
    });

    return { updatedBatch, adjustment };
  });

  return result;
}
```

**Type Safety Benefits:**
- ✅ `remainingQty` must be number, not string
- ✅ `status` only accepts `BatchStatus` enum values
- ✅ `adjustmentType` only accepts `StockAdjustmentType` enum values
- ✅ Compiler catches typos like `remaning` or `statuss`
- ✅ IDE autocomplete shows all available fields

---

## 🗂️ Complete Generated Type Structure

### **Generated Location in node_modules:**
```
node_modules/.prisma/client/
│
├── 📄 index.d.ts (Main file - 5000+ lines)
│   ├── [Import section]
│   │   └── All dependencies from TypeScript
│   │
│   ├── [Enums]
│   │   ├── UserRole
│   │   ├── BatchStatus
│   │   ├── ScanSource
│   │   ├── TransactionIsolationLevel ← ACID!
│   │   └── ... (15+ enums)
│   │
│   ├── [Model Types]
│   │   ├── User
│   │   ├── IngredientBatch
│   │   ├── StockAdjustment
│   │   ├── ScanLog
│   │   ├── Store
│   │   ├── Device
│   │   └── ... (20+ models)
│   │
│   ├── [Input Types]
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchCreateInput
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchUpdateInput
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchWhereInput
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchSelect
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchInclude
│   │   └── ... (for each model × 5 operations)
│   │
│   ├── [Delegates]
│   │   ├── IngredientBatchDelegate
│   │   ├── StockAdjustmentDelegate
│   │   ├── ScanLogDelegate
│   │   └── ... (methods for CRUD operations)
│   │
│   ├── [Prisma Namespace]
│   │   ├── TransactionClient ← ACID TYPE!
│   │   ├── TransactionOptions
│   │   ├── TransactionIsolationLevel ← SERIALIZABLE!
│   │   └── [All nested types...]
│   │
│   └── [PrismaClient Interface]
│       ├── $transaction() ← ACID METHOD!
│       ├── $connect()
│       ├── $disconnect()
│       ├── ingredientBatch
│       ├── stockAdjustment
│       ├── scanLog
│       └── ... (for each model)
│
├── 📄 index.js (Runtime implementation)
│
├── 📁 runtime/
│   ├── index.d.ts
│   ├── library.d.ts
│   ├── query.d.ts
│   └── utils.d.ts
│
└── 📄 prisma-client.d.ts (Client interface)
```

---

## 🔍 Key ACID-Related Types in index.d.ts

### **1. TransactionIsolationLevel Enum**
```typescript
// Location: node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
export namespace Prisma {
  export enum TransactionIsolationLevel {
    ReadUncommitted = "ReadUncommitted",
    ReadCommitted = "ReadCommitted",
    RepeatableRead = "RepeatableRead",
    Serializable = "Serializable"  // ← Used in our code!
  }
}
```

**Usage in PrismaService:**
```typescript
return await this.$transaction(operation, { 
  isolationLevel: Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel.Serializable  
  // ← Type-checked enum from generated index.d.ts
});
```

---

### **2. TransactionClient Type**
```typescript
// Location: node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
export namespace Prisma {
  export interface TransactionClient {
    ingredientBatch: PrismaPromise<IngredientBatchDelegate<GlobalReject>>
    stockAdjustment: PrismaPromise<StockAdjustmentDelegate<GlobalReject>>
    scanLog: PrismaPromise<ScanLogDelegate<GlobalReject>>
    auditLog: PrismaPromise<AuditLogDelegate<GlobalReject>>
    // ... all other models
  }
}
```

**Usage in Stock Adjustment:**
```typescript
await this.prisma.runInTransaction(async (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => {
  // tx has all the same methods as PrismaClient
  // But operations are wrapped in a transaction
  const batch = await tx.ingredientBatch.findUnique({ ... });
  // ↑ tx type is guaranteed to be Prisma.TransactionClient
});
```

---

### **3. Transaction Options Type**
```typescript
// Location: node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
export interface TransactionOptions {
  isolationLevel?: TransactionIsolationLevel
  timeout?: number        // Default: 5000ms
  maxWait?: number       // Default: 2000ms
}
```

**Usage in PrismaService:**
```typescript
async runInTransaction<T>(
  operation: (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => Promise<T>,
  options: TransactionOptions = {}  // ← Type from index.d.ts
): Promise<T> {
  const { isolationLevel = Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel.Serializable } = options;
  // ↑ Type safety: isolationLevel must be TransactionIsolationLevel
}
```

---

## 🔗 How TypeScript Resolves These Types

### **Import Chain:**
```
stock-adjustments.service.ts
  ↓ (imports)
  ├── import { Prisma, BatchStatus } from '@prisma/client'
  ↓ (resolves to)
node_modules/@prisma/client/index.d.ts
  ↓ (re-exports)
node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts  ← ACTUAL TYPE DEFINITIONS!
```

### **File Resolution:**
```
@prisma/client (package)
  ↓
package.json: "typings": "./dist/index.d.ts"
  ↓
dist/index.d.ts: export * from '../.prisma/client'
  ↓
.prisma/client/index.d.ts (auto-generated!)
```

---

## 📋 How to View Generated Types

### **Option 1: View in VS Code**
```
1. Open: node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
2. Hover over any Prisma type to see definition
3. Ctrl+Click to jump to type definition
```

### **Option 2: Search in Terminal**
```bash
# Find all BatchStatus references
grep -n "BatchStatus" node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts | head -20

# Count total lines in generated file
wc -l node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
# Output: ~6000 lines of generated TypeScript

# Search for transaction types
grep -n "TransactionClient\|TransactionIsolationLevel" node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts
```

### **Option 3: Print Type Info**
```typescript
// In any TypeScript file, write:
import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

type BatchType = Prisma.IngredientBatch;
type ClientType = Prisma.TransactionClient;
type IsolationType = Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel;

// Hover over types to see their full definition
```

---

## 🛠️ When Types Are Generated

### **Automatic Generation (happens after each command):**
```bash
npm install @prisma/client       # Generates types
npx prisma migrate dev           # Generates types
npx prisma db seed              # Generates types (if schema changes)
npx prisma generate             # Explicit generation
```

### **Manual Regeneration:**
```bash
npx prisma generate
# Output:
# ✔ Generated Prisma Client (5.22.0) to ./node_modules/.prisma/client
# Time: 234ms
```

### **File Size:**
```
.prisma/client/index.d.ts:  ~150KB  (compressed in npm package)
                            ~6000 lines
                            ~250 interfaces/types
                            ~30 enums
```

---

## ⚠️ Important Notes About Generated Types

### **1. Don't Edit Manually**
❌ **Never** edit `node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts`
- Changes will be lost on `npm install` or `prisma generate`
- Edit `schema.prisma` instead

### **2. Always in node_modules**
❌ **Never** commit `.prisma/` folder to git
- Add to `.gitignore`:
```gitignore
node_modules/
.prisma/
```

### **3. Regenerate After Schema Changes**
```bash
# Schema changed?
npx prisma migrate dev --name descriptive_name
# Types auto-regenerate! ✅

# Or manually:
npx prisma generate
```

---

## 📊 ACID-Related Types Reference

| Type | Location | Purpose |
|------|----------|---------|
| `Prisma.TransactionClient` | index.d.ts | Parameter type for `runInTransaction()` |
| `Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel` | index.d.ts | Enum for isolation levels (SERIALIZABLE) |
| `Prisma.TransactionOptions` | index.d.ts | Options for transaction configuration |
| `Prisma.IngredientBatch` | index.d.ts | Model type for batch operations |
| `Prisma.StockAdjustment` | index.d.ts | Model type for adjustment tracking |
| `Prisma.ScanLog` | index.d.ts | Model type for audit trail |
| `BatchStatus` | index.d.ts | Enum for batch states (ACTIVE, DEPLETED) |
| `StockAdjustmentType` | index.d.ts | Enum for adjustment types (INCREASE, DECREASE) |

---

## 🎯 Summary

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  YOUR SCHEMA (What you write)                           │
│  ============================================            │
│  backend/prisma/schema.prisma                           │
│  ├── model User { ... }                                 │
│  ├── model IngredientBatch { ... }                      │
│  ├── enum BatchStatus { ... }                           │
│  └── ...                                                │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                   │ (npx prisma generate)
                   ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  GENERATED TYPES (What Prisma creates)                  │
│  ============================================            │
│  node_modules/.prisma/client/index.d.ts                 │
│  ├── interface User { ... }                             │
│  ├── interface IngredientBatch { ... }                  │
│  ├── enum BatchStatus { ... }                           │
│  ├── interface TransactionClient { ... } ← ACID!        │
│  ├── enum TransactionIsolationLevel { ... } ← ACID!     │
│  ├── interface PrismaClient {                           │
│  │   $transaction() { ... } ← ACID!                     │
│  └── }                                                  │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                   │ (imported in code)
                   ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  YOUR CODE (What uses types)                            │
│  ============================================            │
│  backend/src/modules/stock-adjustments/               │
│  stock-adjustments.service.ts                           │
│  ├── import { Prisma, BatchStatus } from '@prisma'     │
│  ├── async create(...) {                                │
│  │   const batch: Prisma.IngredientBatch = ...         │
│  │   await prisma.runInTransaction(                    │
│  │     async (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => {       │
│  │       ...type-safe operations...                     │
│  │     }                                                │
│  │   )                                                  │
│  └── }                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Now you know exactly where ACID types come from and how they're used! 🎯
